sound_type: Thunderous body percussion -- humpback whale full-body breach, 25-40 metric tons fully airborne then crashing onto ocean surface, broadband concussive impact, E=9
ecosystem: Open ocean -- NE Pacific pelagic waters, feeding and migration range
milestone: FIRST BALAENOPTERIDAE at degree 278. The rorqual family enters the engine. Third E=9 event in SPS catalog (273: orca breach, 277: orca chorus, 278: humpback breach). Most massive single physical event in catalog: 25-40 metric tons fully airborne. Combined E=17 with Unwound (S36 278, E=8).

coordinates.description: NE Pacific pelagic waters. Washington and BC offshore Pacific. Strait of Juan de Fuca. Summer feeding range. Migration route: Hawaii breeding grounds (winter) to North Pacific feeding grounds (summer).

breach_data.mass_metric_tons: 25-40 metric tons (adult; females average ~28t, males slightly smaller; maximum ~40t)
breach_data.breach_mechanism: Tail fluke propulsion from depth at steep angle. Entire body or nearly entire body exits water. Brief near-total airborne phase. Impact: broadband concussive shockwave through water and air.
breach_data.kinetic_energy_estimate: At 6-8 m/s and 40,000 kg: approximately 720,000-1,280,000 joules at apex. Converts to acoustic and hydrodynamic energy on impact.
breach_data.impact_sound: Broadband. Described by observers as explosive, concussive, physically felt. Hydrophone-detectable at distance. Source level on order of 115-125 dB re 1 µPa @ 1m for sustained impact; peak transient may be higher.
breach_data.serial_breaching: Multiple successive breaches (serial breaching) documented when other whales are nearby. Energetically costly. Suggests significant communicative or social function.
breach_data.vs_orca_breach: Orca breach (degree 273): ~6 metric tons. Humpback breach (degree 278): 25-40 metric tons. Mass ratio approximately 6:1. Both designated E=9.

nodes.title: First Balaenopteridae -- Rorqual Family Enters
nodes.description: Degree 278 is the first entry in the Balaenopteridae family (rorqual whales). The family includes the largest animals in Earth's history. Humpback (278) begins a Balaenopteridae arc that continues with blue whale (279).

nodes.title: Third E=9 Event -- SPS Maximum Tier
nodes.description: SPS now has three E=9 events: orca breach (273, physical), orca multi-pod chorus (277, social), humpback breach (278, physical). Two physical maxima and one social maximum. The E=9 tier is established.

nodes.title: Mass Maximum -- Largest Physical Event
nodes.description: 25-40 metric tons fully airborne is the largest mass in any single physical event in the SPS catalog. Orca breach is ~6 tons. Humpback is 6x heavier. The mass scaling does not change the E=9 designation -- E=9 is a qualitative ceiling.

cross_references.relationship: First physical E=9. Orca ~6 tons vs. humpback ~40 tons. Same designation, very different mass. E=9 as qualitative ceiling confirmed.

Second Balaenopteridae. The blue whale: largest animal in Earth's history. E=5. Balaenopteridae arc: E=9 spectacular breach (humpback) to E=5 infrasonic depth (blue whale). Maximum spectacle to maximum depth.
Social E=9. Three E=9 events now in catalog: two physical (273, 278), one social (277). SPS maximum tier established.

Combined E=17. Angular post-hardcore severity (Unwound, E=8) paired with massive physical force (humpback breach, E=9). Both accumulative forces. First Balaenopteridae alongside Olympia post-hardcore legend.
